LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MIKHAIL GORELIK'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (REQUEST TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED AS TO ARKADY GORELIK. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 425.16. THE CENTRAL ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT ARE SET FORTH IN PARAGRAPHS 6, 7, 8, 10, 22, 47 AND 52. THIS IS A DISPUTE ABOUT PROPERTY. THE ONLY CONDUCT ALLEGED THAT CONSTITUTES A DEPRIVATION OF PROPERTY IS SET OUT IN PARAGRAPH TEN AND REFERENCES THE FIVE DAY NOTICE AND UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION. IN ESSENCE, PLAINTIFF CONTENDS THAT DEFENDANT HAS ENGAGED IN ELDER ABUSE BY SEEKING TO DEPRIVE HIS FATHER OF HIS INTEREST IN REAL PROPERTY BY DEFENDANT ASSERTING A LEGAL CLAIM TO THE PROPERTY. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE A TIMELY FACTUAL RESPONSE AS TO PRONG TWO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
